chunks.title: Course Information
chunks.content:
Course Number: CSCE 411
Course Title: Design and Analysis of Algorithms
Section: 500
Time: 3:00-3:50pm on Mondays, Wednesdays and Fridays
Location: 106 EABB
Credit Hours: 3

chunks.title: Instructor Details
chunks.content:
Instructor: Anxiao Jiang
Office: 425 PETR
Phone: 979-845-7983
E-Mail: ajiang@cse.tamu.edu
Office Hours: 9:00-9:30am on Mondays via zoom

chunks.title: Course Description
chunks.content: The course covers fundamental techniques for analyzing and designing algorithms, including dynamic programming, greedy algorithms, amortized analysis, graph algorithms, maximum flow, linear programming, NP-completeness, approximation algorithms, and online algorithms.

chunks.title: Course Prerequisites
chunks.content: Grade of C or better in CSCE 221 and CSCE 222/ECEN 222; junior or senior classification or approval of instructor.

chunks.title: Course Learning Outcomes
chunks.content:
Upon successful completion of the course, students will be able to:

1. Explain a variety of fundamental algorithms and algorithmic techniques
2. Select the most appropriate algorithm to solve a given problem
3. Analyze the correctness, runtime, and space complexity of an algorithm
4. Design new algorithms and modify existing ones using techniques discussed in class
5. Recognize when a problem is likely intractable (has no efficient algorithm) and be able to prove this by showing undecidability or NP-hardness

chunks.title: Textbook and/or Resource Materials
chunks.content:
Recommended Textbook

*   Introduction to Algorithms, Fourth Edition, By Thomas H. Corman, Charles E. Leiserson, Ronald L. Rivest and Clifford Stein, MIT Press.

chunks.title: Grading Policy
chunks.content:
Ten to twelve homework assignments: 40%
Two projects: 30% each (60% total)

Grading Scale: A = [90, 100], B = [80, 90), C = [70, 80), D = [60, 70), F = [0, 60)

chunks.title: Late Work Policy
chunks.content: With the exception of excused absences (see Student Rule 7), no homework or project submitted after the established deadline will be accepted.

warnings: Specific start/end dates for the semester are not provided, only project due dates.
